Title: OsbHLH057 targets the AATCA cis-element to regulate disease resistance and drought tolerance in rice

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: The AATCA motif recognized by OsbHLH057 is similar to the canonical bHLH binding site WATCW (W = A/T). Phylogenetic analysis groups OsbHLH057 with Arabidopsis subgroup IV bHLH transcription factors such as AtbHLH34, AtbHLH104, AtbHLH105 (ILR3), and AtbHLH115; OsbHLH60 is its closest paralog in rice. Overexpression of OsbHLH057 does not alter plant height, tiller number, aboveground or underground biomass at 70 days.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of seedling leaves from WT and OsbHLH057 overexpression plants; data deposited in NCBI SRA under accession PRJNA736611.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as a positive regulator of rice resistance to R. solani and Xoo and of drought tolerance; overexpression lines show shorter disease lesions, reduced pathogen biomass, enhanced antioxidant enzyme activities and lower H2O2 under drought, whereas knockout lines are more susceptible and accumulate more H2O2; altered expression does not detectably affect plant growth and development under normal conditions.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: Nuclear bHLH transcription factor that binds the AATCA cis-element in target promoters and activates transcription; directly activates Os2H16 via the AATCA element in its promoter; overexpression increases expression of defense-related PR genes (OsPR1a, OsPR10) and drought-associated marker genes (OsOSE2, OsDip1) under stress, enhances SOD, POD and CAT activities, and reduces H2O2 accumulation during drought.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Functions in rice defense and drought-response pathways by activating AATCA-motif-containing genes, including Os2H16 and many defense- and hormone-related genes, thereby enhancing responses to pathogens and drought.
Plant_Genes.Upstream_or_Downstream_Regulation: OsbHLH057 --(transcriptional activation via binding to AATCA cis-element in promoter)--> Os2H16; OsbHLH057 --(positive regulation inferred from expression changes in OE/KO lines under stress)--> OsPR1a, OsPR10, OsOSE2, OsDip1.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Basic helix–loop–helix (bHLH) transcription factor; contains a bHLH domain (residues 93–144) and a leucine zipper (ZIP) motif (residues 150–185); placed in bHLH subfamily IV by phylogenetic analysis.
Plant_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Nucleus, determined by OsbHLH057–GFP fusion transiently expressed in Nicotiana benthamiana epidermal cells and colocalization with DAPI staining.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: OsbHLH057 transcript is rapidly induced by R. solani infection (peak at 8 h), by Xoo infection (rapid induction at 4 h, peak at 6 h), and by 15% (w/v) PEG8000 osmotic stress (strong induction at 24 h).
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Inoculation with Rhizoctonia solani and Xanthomonas oryzae pv. oryzae; 15% (w/v) PEG8000 treatment to mimic drought; soil drought by withholding water for 21 days.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Transcriptional regulation of disease resistance and drought tolerance in rice
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Biotic stress (sheath blight caused by Rhizoctonia solani, bacterial blight caused by Xanthomonas oryzae pv. oryzae); Abiotic stress (drought, PEG-induced osmotic stress)
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: After R. solani inoculation, OsbHLH057 OE lines show lesion lengths of approximately 1.8–4.5 cm, KO lines 4.6–7.4 cm, versus about 4.7 cm in WT. After Xoo inoculation, OE lines show an average reduction in lesion length of about 3.7 cm and KO lines an average increase of about 2.3 cm compared with WT (~8.95 cm). Under drought, water loss from detached leaves is slower in OE and faster in KO plants than in WT; PEG8000-induced drought elevates SOD, POD and CAT activities in OE and reduces them in KO relative to WT, with corresponding decreases and increases in H2O2 content respectively.
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Overexpression reduces seed germination whereas knockout lines set seeds normally, indicating specific roles in seed germination distinct from OsASR2.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Oryza sativa cv. Zhonghua 11 transgenic overexpression (OE) lines expressing OsbHLH057 under the pCXUN-HA vector; CRISPR/Cas9-generated OsbHLH057 knockout (KO) lines; wild-type Zhonghua 11; Nicotiana benthamiana leaves for transient expression assays.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: DNA pull-down with AATCA probe and mass spectrometry to identify AATCA-binding proteins; cloning of OsbHLH057 CDS into pCXUN-HA for overexpression and into CRISPR/Cas9 vectors for knockout; Agrobacterium-mediated rice transformation; qRT-PCR for expression analysis; EMSA to test binding of OsbHLH057 to AATCA; ChIP-qPCR in OE plants to confirm in vivo binding; GFP reporter assays with AATCA-35S mini constructs in N. benthamiana and transgenic rice; GFP fusion and confocal microscopy for subcellular localization; yeast transcriptional activation assay (Y1H) in strain AH109; sheath inoculation with R. solani and leaf inoculation with Xoo for disease assays; qPCR quantification of pathogen biomass; drought and PEG8000 treatments, water-loss measurements, and biochemical assays of SOD, POD, CAT activities and H2O2 content; RNA-seq of WT and OsbHLH057 OE seedlings followed by mapping, differential expression, and GO enrichment analyses.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Functional validation by generating OsbHLH057 overexpression and CRISPR/Cas9 knockout rice lines and assaying responses to R. solani, Xoo, and drought/PEG treatments; EMSA and ChIP-qPCR to demonstrate specific binding to the AATCA cis-element in the Os2H16 promoter; AATCA-driven GFP reporter assays coexpressed with OsbHLH057 to show transcriptional activation; yeast transcriptional activation assay and nuclear localization analysis to confirm its role as a transcription factor.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Identified as an AATCA-binding protein by DNA pull-down assay using an AATCA-containing probe followed by mass spectrometry; corresponding gene (LOC_Os07g35870) cloned for overexpression and CRISPR/Cas9 knockout.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: OsbHLH057 is a promising target for engineering rice with enhanced resistance to sheath blight and bacterial blight and improved drought tolerance, as its overexpression strengthens defense and drought responses without detectable penalties on plant growth and yield-related traits under normal conditions; the OsbHLH057–AATCA module offers a pathogen- and drought-inducible regulatory system to drive defense genes such as Os2H16, potentially reducing resistance costs associated with constitutive expression.

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Os2H16 encodes a 61-amino-acid peptide with multifaceted resistance functions against sheath blight, bacterial blight, and drought as previously characterized; it is specifically expressed in anthers, and the OsASR2–GT-1 and OsbHLH057–AATCA modules converge on its promoter to coordinate biotic and abiotic stress responses.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: Os2H16 is among the defense-related genes upregulated in OsbHLH057 overexpression plants identified by RNA-seq; RNA-seq data are available in NCBI SRA under accession PRJNA736611.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Os2H16 is a multifaceted defense-related gene encoding a 61-amino-acid peptide; its expression is transcriptionally activated via the AATCA cis-element in its promoter bound by OsbHLH057, and increased Os2H16 expression in OsbHLH057 overexpression lines correlates with enhanced resistance to R. solani and Xoo and improved drought tolerance, while reduced Os2H16 expression in OsbHLH057 knockout lines correlates with increased susceptibility.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: Its promoter contains two R. solani-inducible fragments: a −411 to −309 region with a GT-1 element and a −513 to −412 region harboring the AATCA cis-element. Deletion or mutation of the AATCA motif abolishes pathogen-inducible GFP expression, while tandem AATCA motifs confer strong R. solani- and Xoo-inducible expression when fused to a minimal promoter. OsbHLH057 binds specifically to the AATCA-containing region, as shown by EMSA and ChIP-qPCR, and activates Os2H16 transcription in vivo.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Os2H16 functions in rice defense and drought-response pathways as a downstream effector gene whose promoter integrates inputs from at least two cis-elements: the GT-1 element, bound by OsASR2 (from previous work), and the AATCA cis-element, bound by OsbHLH057, thereby contributing to resistance to R. solani, Xoo, and drought.
Plant_Genes.Upstream_or_Downstream_Regulation: OsbHLH057 --(transcriptional activation via binding to AATCA cis-element in promoter)--> Os2H16.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Promoter analysis shows that Os2H16 carries two R. solani-inducible regions: a −411 to −309 fragment containing GT-1 and a −513 to −412 fragment containing the newly defined AATCA cis-element; the AATCA element is required and sufficient (when repeated and fused to a minimal promoter) for strong R. solani- and Xoo-inducible reporter expression in transgenic rice. In OsbHLH057 OE lines, Os2H16 transcript levels are significantly higher, and in OsbHLH057 KO lines significantly lower, than in WT plants after R. solani, Xoo, or PEG8000 treatment.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Inoculation with Rhizoctonia solani and Xanthomonas oryzae pv. oryzae; treatments with chitin, Xanthomonas oryzae pv. oryzicola, and heat-treated R. solani to test AATCA responsiveness; 15% (w/v) PEG8000 to mimic drought.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Stress-responsive small peptide gene whose promoter cis-elements and regulation by OsbHLH057 control disease resistance and drought tolerance in rice
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Biotic stress (sheath blight caused by Rhizoctonia solani, bacterial blight caused by Xanthomonas oryzae pv. oryzae); Abiotic stress (drought, PEG-induced osmotic stress)
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Os2H16 promoter fragments fused to GFP reporter constructs (including Del.1–Del.5 and Del.6–Del.10) in N. benthamiana; Os2H16 promoter constructs with or without the AATCA element and 2×AATCA-35S mini constructs in transgenic rice cv. Zhonghua 11; OsbHLH057 OE and KO rice lines used to examine Os2H16 expression.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Promoter deletion analysis of the Os2H16 upstream region (−513 to +60) using GFP reporters in N. benthamiana and transgenic rice; fine deletion of the −513 to −484 region into overlapping 10-bp fragments fused to 35S minimal promoter to localize the R. solani-inducible motif; targeted deletion and mutagenesis of the AATCA motif (conversion to GCCAC) within the Os2H16 promoter; generation of transgenic rice lines harboring 2×AATCA-35S mini and 2×GCCAC-35S mini constructs; R. solani and Xoo inoculation and chitin or heat-treated pathogen treatments to test inducibility; EMSA and ChIP-qPCR to confirm OsbHLH057 binding to the AATCA-containing promoter region; qRT-PCR analysis of Os2H16 expression in OsbHLH057 OE and KO lines under pathogen and PEG8000 treatments.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Functional analysis of Os2H16 regulatory elements by promoter deletion and motif mutagenesis fused to GFP in transient N. benthamiana assays and stable transgenic rice, combined with R. solani and Xoo inoculation; duplication of the AATCA motif upstream of a minimal promoter to test sufficiency for pathogen inducibility; EMSA and ChIP-qPCR to show that OsbHLH057 binds the AATCA-containing region of the Os2H16 promoter and activates Os2H16 expression in overexpression lines while knockout of OsbHLH057 suppresses Os2H16 induction under stress.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Previously identified as a multifaceted defense-related gene encoding a 61-amino-acid peptide; in this study, its promoter was dissected to identify R. solani- and Xoo-inducible cis-elements including the AATCA motif.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Os2H16, together with its AATCA-containing promoter, represents a useful module for engineering rice with enhanced resistance to sheath blight and bacterial blight and improved drought tolerance. The AATCA cis-element confers strong R. solani- and Xoo-inducible expression when fused to a minimal promoter, suggesting that Os2H16 or other defense genes driven by AATCA-based synthetic promoters could provide inducible resistance with reduced fitness costs compared with constitutive promoters.
